attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
- commit-optimizer: Create structured optimization commit messages with measured performance/size results. Use this whenever you're committing code optimizations (especially RISC-V libm assembly tuning) and want to automatically benchmark the changes and generate a properly formatted commit message with problem, approach, and result sections. Invoke with `/commit`.
- libm-testpoints: IEEE 754 numerical analysis expert for generating representative libm test points. Use this skill whenever the user asks to generate test points, test cases, or test inputs for math library (libm) functions (sin, cos, exp, log, sqrt, pow, etc.), whether float or double precision. Also trigger when the user wants to improve, audit, or extend existing test_*.c files in the RISC-V libm test harness. Trigger on requests like "generate 100 test points for sinf", "what are the key test cases for expf?", or "add representative tests to test_logf.c". The argument is the function name, e.g. "/libm-testpoints sinf" or "/libm-testpoints sin".
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions on AX45MPV (VLEN=1024). Runs a 7-step FSM — select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, profile, learn — then loops back to analyze with new pipeline insights. No human intervention required.
- wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /optimize learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session

content.content: # Keyword spotting for Microcontrollers This repository consists of the tensorflow models and training scripts used in the paper: [Hello Edge: Keyword spotting on Microcontrollers](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1711.07128.pdf). The scripts are adapted from [Tensorflow examples](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/tree/master/tensorflow/examples/speech_commands) and some are repeated here for the sake of making these scripts self-contained. To train a DNN with 3 fully-connected layers with 128 neurons in each layer, run: ``` python train.py --model_architecture dnn --model_size_info 128 128 128 ``` The command line argument *--model_size_info* is used to pass the neural network layer dimensions such as number of layers, convolution filter size/stride as a list to models.py, which builds the tensorflow graph based on the provided model architecture and layer dimensions. For more info on *model_size_info* for each network architecture see [models.py](models.py). The training commands with all the hyperparameters to reproduce the models shown in the [paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1711.07128.pdf) are given [here](train_commands.txt). To run inference on the trained model from a checkpoint on train/val/test set, run: ``` python test.py --model_architecture dnn --model_size_info 128 128 128 --checkpoint ``` To freeze the trained model checkpoint into a .pb file, run: ``` python freeze.py --model_architecture dnn --model_size_info 128 128 128 --checkpoint --output_file dnn.pb ``` ## Pretrained models Trained models (.pb files) for different neural network architectures such as DNN, CNN, Basic LSTM, LSTM, GRU, CRNN and DS-CNN shown in this [arXiv paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1711.07128.pdf) are added in [Pretrained_models](Pretrained_models). Accuracy of the models on validation set, their memory requirements and operations per inference are also summarized in the following table. To run an audio file through the trained model (e.g. a DNN) and get top prediction, run: ``` python label_wav.py --wav

content.text:
ML-KWS-for-MCU is a keyword spotting suite containing **7 model architectures** from the "Hello Edge" paper (arXiv:1711.07128):

- **DNN** - 3 fully-connected layers
- **CNN** - convolutional
- **Basic LSTM** / **LSTM** - recurrent
- **GRU** - recurrent
- **CRNN** - conv + recurrent
- **DS-CNN** - depthwise-separable CNN (best accuracy/size tradeoff per the paper)

Input: MFCC features from 1-second audio; output: keyword class. Pretrained `.pb` files live in `Pretrained_models/<ARCH>/`. The Deployment/ subfolder has the DNN example ported to MCU using CMSIS-NN-style kernels.

cifar10
gru
# CIFAR-10 Baseline Performance

**Board:** sw-boards.andestech.com:1115 (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
**Date:** 2026-04-13
**Build:** `-O3 -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DFAST_VERSION`

## Total

| Inst | Cycle |
|---|---|
| 715,518 | 1,354,565 |

## Per-layer

| Layer | Function | Inst | Cycle |
|---|---|---|---|
| conv1 RGB (32x32, 3->32ch) | `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_RGB_sft_bias_fast` | 465,967 | 967,797 |
| relu1 | `riscv_nn_relu_s8` | 163 | 2,365 |
| pool1 (32->16) | `riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8` | 22,889 | 34,424 |
| conv2 (16x16, 32->32ch) | `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | 162,536 | 246,845 |
| relu2 | `riscv_nn_relu_s8` | 37 | 153 |
| pool2 (16->8) | `riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8` | 5,721 | 8,011 |
| conv3 (8x8, 32->64ch) | `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | 56,185 | 87,923 |
| relu3 | `riscv_nn_relu_s8` | 32 | 92 |
| pool3 (8->4) | `riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8` | 1,457 | 2,087 |
| fc (256->10) | `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | 500 | 13,774 |
| softmax | `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast` | 68 | 351 |
################################################################################
# CIFAR-10 CNN example — build and run on Andes QEMU / FPGA (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
#
#   make            — build cifar10.elf (QEMU/correctness)
#   make run        — run on QEMU (strace mode, captures semihosting output)
#   make perf       — build and run on FPGA, report total inst+cycle count
#   make perf-layers — build and run on FPGA, report per-layer inst+cycle breakdown
#   make clean      — remove build artifacts
################################################################################

LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc

QEMU := /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
QEMU_CFG := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
VLEN := 1024
QEMU_TIMEOUT := 30

BOARD_WRAPPER := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
export MYPC  ?= sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT ?= 1115

BUILD_DIR := build
ELF       := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10.elf
ELF_PERF  := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_perf.elf
ELF_LAYERS := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_layers.elf
SRC_ORIG  := nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
SRC_PATCHED := $(BUILD_DIR)/nds_nn_examples_cifar10_patched.c

CFLAGS := -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections
CFLAGS += -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS
CFLAGS += -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=$(VLEN)
CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION

INCS := -I$(BUILD_DIR)/shim
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Include
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/internal
INCS += -I.
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Examples/unit_func/include

LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm

# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
	softmax.h activation.h pooling.h fully_connected.h convolution.h)

# Extra flags shared by both perf builds
PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP

.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean

all: $(ELF)

$(ELF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

# Patch: add missing batch=1 argument to riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8 calls
$(SRC_PATCHED): $(SRC_ORIG) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	sed \
	  -e 's/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\(img_buffer[12]\), \(CONV[123]_OUT_DIM\)/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\1, 1, \2/g' \
	  $< > $@

# Generate shim headers
$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
	echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
	mkdir -p $@

$(BUILD_DIR):
[rerun: b7]

LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm

# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
	softmax.h activation.h pooling.h fully_connected.h convolution.h)

# Extra flags shared by both perf builds
PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP

.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean

all: $(ELF)

$(ELF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

# Patch: add missing batch=1 argument to riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8 calls
$(SRC_PATCHED): $(SRC_ORIG) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	sed \
	  -e 's/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\(img_buffer[12]\), \(CONV[123]_OUT_DIM\)/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\1, 1, \2/g' \
	  $< > $@

# Generate shim headers
$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
	echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
	mkdir -p $@

$(BUILD_DIR):

2. Which CMSIS-NN functions does the DNN model call? (grep #include and function names from main.cpp and Source/)
3. What's the model architecture exactly (layer sizes, operator sequence)? Look at dnn.cpp / model weights in Source/
4. What's needed to adapt it for libnn like cifar10 was (shim headers, function renames, perf counter hooks)? Check how cifar10/Makefile patches the code.

content.text:
Now I have enough information. Let me create my report:

## Report: ML-KWS-for-MCU DNN Port to libnn

**1. Structure of ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/**

The simple_test (non-mbed) variant is minimal:
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Examples/simple_test/main.cpp` — entry point using mbed abstractions (Timer, Serial, USBTX/USBRX)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Examples/simple_test/wav_data.h` — test audio data

Real implementation in Source/:
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp/h` — DNN model class (4 fully-connected layers)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn_weights.h` — 319KB weights/biases
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/KWS/` — wrapper (KWS/KWS_DNN classes)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/MFCC/mfcc.cpp` — feature extraction
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/local_NN/` — custom custom pooling (arm_avepool_q7_HWC_nonsquare.c)

Yes, CMSIS-NN based: includes `arm_nnfunctions.h`, `arm_math.h`.

**2. CMSIS-NN Functions Called**

DNN layer functions (from `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp` lines 75-90):
- 4x `arm_fully_connected_q7()` (4 FC layers IP1-IP4)
- 3x `arm_relu_q7()` (activations after IP1, IP2, IP3)
- Softmax added later in KWS pipeline (`arm_softmax_q7()` in `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/KWS/kws.cpp`)

**3. Model Architecture**

Defined in dnn.h/dnn.cpp:
- Input: 10x25 MFCC features (250 dims, int8 quantized)
- IP1: 250→144 (weights: 36K, bias_shift=1, out_rshift=7)
- IP2: 144→144 (weights: 20.7K, bias_shift=2, out_rshift=8)
- IP3: 144→144 (weights: 20.7K, bias_shift=2, out_rshift=9)
- IP4: 144→12 (weights: 1.7K, bias_shift=0, out_rshift=6)
- Output: 12 classes (silence, unknown, yes, no, up, down, left, right, on, off, stop, go)

**4. Adaptation Needs for libnn**

Per cifar10/Makefile pattern:
- Create shim headers: `/build/shim/arm_nn_*.h` → `#include "riscv_nn_*.h"` (activation.h, fully_connected.h)
- Patch dnn.cpp to rename CMSIS calls: `arm_fully_connected_q7()` → `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast()` (q7→s8 semantics differ; needs signature rework)
- Add PF_COUNTER instrumentation hooks around `run_nn()` calls for baseline measurement
- Remove mbed dependencies from simple_test/main.cpp (Timer, Serial, USBTX/USBRX)

**5. Non-mbed Variant Status**

simple_test/main.cpp is almost standalone but depends on mbed:
- `#include "mbed.h"` implied by `Timer T`, `Serial pc(USBTX, USBRX)` 
- Would need stub implementations or removal for libnn target

**6. CMSIS→libnn Gotchas**

Key signature mismatch vs cifar10:
- `arm_fully_connected_q7(input, weights, in_dim, out_dim, bias_lshift, out_rshift, bias, output, vec_buf)` — 9 args
- libnn `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast()` — different parameter order/types (q7=int8, shifts as separate args)
- No batch dimension in original (implicit batch=1); libnn functions may require explicit batch parameter
- Local custom pooling in Source/local_NN/ uses non-standard signature and won't have libnn equivalent

input.content:
# Plan: KWS-DNN baseline on AX45MPV FPGA

## Context

User wants baseline FPGA cycle counts for the ML-KWS-for-MCU DNN model (the largest model suite in `Examples/`). Goal is to set up a reproducible build+profile flow mirroring the existing cifar10/gru ports under `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/`, producing a `baseline.md` with total and per-layer inst/cycle numbers for future libnn RVV optimization work.

The DNN model is a 4-layer FC keyword-spotting net from the "Hello Edge" paper. CMSIS-NN calls used (`arm_fully_connected_q7`, `arm_relu_q7`, `arm_softmax_q7`) have byte-identical argument lists and semantics to libnn's shift-based `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` / `riscv_nn_relu_s8` / `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast`, so porting is a rename, not a signature rewrite.

## Target layout

Create `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/kws_dnn/` following the cifar10 pattern:

```
kws_dnn/
  Makefile                     # clone of cifar10/Makefile
  nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c    # new standalone driver (no mbed)
  dnn_weights.h                # copied from Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/
  baseline.md                  # results (written after run)
```

## Driver source (`nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c`)

Replicates `Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp` inference in plain C:

- Constants from `dnn.h`: `IN_DIM=250`, `OUT_DIM=12`, `IP1_OUT=IP2_OUT=IP3_OUT=144`, shifts (`IP1_BIAS_LSHIFT=1,IP1_OUT_RSHIFT=7`; `IP2 2/8`; `IP3 2/9`; `IP4 0/6`).
- Input: static MFCC buffer (250 int8). Use pre-computed MFCC from `Deployment/Examples/simple_test/wav_data.h` or zero-fill (baseline cycle count is data-independent for dense FC).
- Call sequence:
  1. `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast(in, IP1_WT, 250, 144, 1, 7, IP1_BIAS, fc1_out, vec_buf)`
  2. `riscv_nn_relu_s8(fc1_out, 144)`
  3. FC 144->144 (IP2), ReLU
  4. FC 144->144 (IP3), ReLU
  5. FC 144->12 (IP4)
  6. `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast(fc4_out, 1, 12, out)`
- `vec_buf` q15 scratch sized `2 * max(in_dim)` = 500 q15 elements.

Perf instrumentation: wrap each layer with `startPFM()/stopPFM()` macros guarded by `-DPF_COUNTER`, matching cifar10's pattern (see `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` once the Makefile patches it).

## Makefile

Copy `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile` verbatim, change:
- `SRC_ORIG := nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c`
- `ELF := build/kws_dnn.elf` (and perf/layers variants)
- Drop the `maxpool` sed patch (no maxpool in DNN); keep the shim-header generation (needs `nds_nn_fully_connected.h`, `nds_nn_activation.h`, `nds_nn_softmax.h`).

Targets: `make` (QEMU build), `make run` (QEMU validation), `make perf` (FPGA total), `make perf-layers` (FPGA per-layer).

## Critical files to reference

- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile` - template
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` - perf-counter instrumentation style
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/baseline.md` - output format
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp` - layer sequence/shifts
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn_weights.h` - 319KB weight arrays (copy as-is)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Include/riscv_nn_fully_connected.h:241` - `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` signature (matches arm_fully_connected_q7)

## Verification

1. Build libnn: `/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh AX45MPV` (ast530 toolchain on PATH).
2. QEMU correctness: `cd Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/kws_dnn && make run` - expect completion without semihosting errors (the DNN output prediction can be spot-checked but is not required for cycle baseline).
3. FPGA total: `make perf` - reports total inst+cycle.
4. FPGA per-layer: `make perf-layers` - prints 8 rows (4 FC, 3 ReLU, 1 softmax).
5. Record numbers in `kws_dnn/baseline.md` matching cifar10/baseline.md format (Total table + per-layer table with libnn function names). Commit with `[misc]` tag.

Note: FPGA run requires board reservation via `MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com MYPORT=1115` (defaults already in cifar10 Makefile). If the board is unavailable, QEMU run still validates correctness but does not yield cycle counts.

# Plan: KWS-DNN baseline on AX45MPV FPGA

## Context

User wants baseline FPGA cycle counts for the ML-KWS-for-MCU DNN model (the largest model suite in `Examples/`). Goal is to set up a reproducible build+profile flow mirroring the existing cifar10/gru ports under `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/`, producing a `baseline.md` with total and per-layer inst/cycle numbers for future libnn RVV optimization work.

The DNN model is a 4-layer FC keyword-spotting net from the "Hello Edge" paper. CMSIS-NN calls used (`arm_fully_connected_q7`, `arm_relu_q7`, `arm_softmax_q7`) have byte-identical argument lists and semantics to libnn's shift-based `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` / `riscv_nn_relu_s8` / `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast`, so porting is a rename, not a signature rewrite.

## Target layout

Create `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/kws_dnn/` following the cifar10 pattern:

```
kws_dnn/
  Makefile                     # clone of cifar10/Makefile
  nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c    # new standalone driver (no mbed)
  dnn_weights.h                # copied from Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/
  baseline.md                  # results (written after run)
```

## Driver source (`nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c`)

Replicates `Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp` inference in plain C:

- Constants from `dnn.h`: `IN_DIM=250`, `OUT_DIM=12`, `IP1_OUT=IP2_OUT=IP3_OUT=144`, shifts (`IP1_BIAS_LSHIFT=1,IP1_OUT_RSHIFT=7`; `IP2 2/8`; `IP3 2/9`; `IP4 0/6`).
- Input: static MFCC buffer (250 int8). Use pre-computed MFCC from `Deployment/Examples/simple_test/wav_data.h` or zero-fill (baseline cycle count is data-independent for dense FC).
- Call sequence:
  1. `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast(in, IP1_WT, 250, 144, 1, 7, IP1_BIAS, fc1_out, vec_buf)`
  2. `riscv_nn_relu_s8(fc1_out, 144)`
  3. FC 144->144 (IP2), ReLU
  4. FC 144->144 (IP3), ReLU
  5. FC 144->12 (IP4)
  6. `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast(fc4_out, 1, 12, out)`
- `vec_buf` q15 scratch sized `2 * max(in_dim)` = 500 q15 elements.

Perf instrumentation: wrap each layer with `startPFM()/stopPFM()` macros guarded by `-DPF_COUNTER`, matching cifar10's pattern (see `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` once the Makefile patches it).

## Makefile

Copy `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile` verbatim, change:
- `SRC_ORIG := nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c`
- `ELF := build/kws_dnn.elf` (and perf/layers variants)
- Drop the `maxpool` sed patch (no maxpool in DNN); keep the shim-header generation (needs `nds_nn_fully_connected.h`, `nds_nn_activation.h`, `nds_nn_softmax.h`).

Targets: `make` (QEMU build), `make run` (QEMU validation), `make perf` (FPGA total), `make perf-layers` (FPGA per-layer).

## Critical files to reference

- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile` - template
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` - perf-counter instrumentation style
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/baseline.md` - output format
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp` - layer sequence/shifts
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn_weights.h` - 319KB weight arrays (copy as-is)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Include/riscv_nn_fully_connected.h:241` - `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` signature (matches arm_fully_connected_q7)

## Verification

1. Build libnn: `/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh AX45MPV` (ast530 toolchain on PATH).
2. QEMU correctness: `cd Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/kws_dnn && make run` - expect completion without semihosting errors (the DNN output prediction can be spot-checked but is not required for cycle baseline).
3. FPGA total: `make perf` - reports total inst+cycle.
4. FPGA per-layer: `make perf-layers` - prints 8 rows (4 FC, 3 ReLU, 1 softmax).
5. Record numbers in `kws_dnn/baseline.md` matching cifar10/baseline.md format (Total table + per-layer table with libnn function names). Commit with `[misc]` tag.

Note: FPGA run requires board reservation via `MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com MYPORT=1115` (defaults already in cifar10 Makefile). If the board is unavailable, QEMU run still validates correctness but does not yield cycle counts.

1	################################################################################
2	# CIFAR-10 CNN example — build and run on Andes QEMU / FPGA (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
3	#
4	#   make            — build cifar10.elf (QEMU/correctness)
5	#   make run        — run on QEMU (strace mode, captures semihosting output)
6	#   make perf       — build and run on FPGA, report total inst+cycle count
7	#   make perf-layers — build and run on FPGA, report per-layer inst+cycle breakdown
8	#   make clean      — remove build artifacts
9	################################################################################
10	
11	LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
12	TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
13	CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc
14	
15	QEMU := /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
16	QEMU_CFG := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
17	VLEN := 1024
18	QEMU_TIMEOUT := 30
19	
20	BOARD_WRAPPER := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
21	export MYPC  ?= sw-boards.andestech.com
22	export MYPORT ?= 1115
23	
24	BUILD_DIR := build
25	ELF       := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10.elf
26	ELF_PERF  := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_perf.elf
27	ELF_LAYERS := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_layers.elf
28	SRC_ORIG  := nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
29	SRC_PATCHED := $(BUILD_DIR)/nds_nn_examples_cifar10_patched.c
30	
31	CFLAGS := -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections
32	CFLAGS += -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS
33	CFLAGS += -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
34	CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=$(VLEN)
35	CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION
36	
37	INCS := -I$(BUILD_DIR)/shim
38	INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Include
39	INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/internal
40	INCS += -I.
41	INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Examples/unit_func/include
42	
43	LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm
44	
45	# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
46	SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
47		softmax.h activation.h pooling.h fully_connected.h convolution.h)
48	
49	# Extra flags shared by both perf builds
50	PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP
51	
52	.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean
53	
54	all: $(ELF)
55	
56	$(ELF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
57		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
58		@echo "Built: $@"
59	
60	# Patch: add missing batch=1 argument to riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8 calls
61	$(SRC_PATCHED): $(SRC_ORIG) | $(BUILD_DIR)
62		sed \
63		  -e 's/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\(img_buffer[12]\), \(CONV[123]_OUT_DIM\)/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\1, 1, \2/g' \
64		  $< > $@
65	
66	# Generate shim headers
67	$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
68		echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@
69	
70	$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
71		mkdir -p $@
72	
73	$(BUILD_DIR):
74		mkdir -p $@
75	
76	run: $(ELF)
77		@echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=$(VLEN) (timeout=$(QEMU_TIMEOUT)s, strace mode)"
78		@STRACE_LOG=$(BUILD_DIR)/strace.log; \
79		strace -f -e trace=write -o $$STRACE_LOG \
80		  $(QEMU) -readconfig $(QEMU_CFG) -M andes_ae350 \
81		  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=$(VLEN) \
82		  -nographic -semihosting -bios $(ELF) > /dev/null 2>&1 & \
83		SPID=$$!; \
84		sleep $(QEMU_TIMEOUT); \
85		kill $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
86		wait $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
87		echo "[output]"; \
88		grep -E 'write\((9|1),' $$STRACE_LOG \
89		  | sed "s/.*write([0-9]*, \"//; s/\\\\n\".*//; s/\".*//"
90	
91	$(ELF_PERF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
92		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
93		@echo "Built: $@"
94	
95	$(ELF_LAYERS): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
96		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
97		@echo "Built: $@"
98	
99	perf: $(ELF_PERF)
100		@echo "[perf] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
101		@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
102		PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_PERF)
103	
104	perf-layers: $(ELF_LAYERS)
105		@echo "[perf-layers] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
106		@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
107		PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_LAYERS)
108	
109	clean:
110		rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

################################################################################
# CIFAR-10 CNN example — build and run on Andes QEMU / FPGA (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
#
#   make            — build cifar10.elf (QEMU/correctness)
#   make run        — run on QEMU (strace mode, captures semihosting output)
#   make perf       — build and run on FPGA, report total inst+cycle count
#   make perf-layers — build and run on FPGA, report per-layer inst+cycle breakdown
#   make clean      — remove build artifacts
################################################################################

LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc

QEMU := /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
QEMU_CFG := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
VLEN := 1024
QEMU_TIMEOUT := 30

BOARD_WRAPPER := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
export MYPC  ?= sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT ?= 1115

BUILD_DIR := build
ELF       := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10.elf
ELF_PERF  := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_perf.elf
ELF_LAYERS := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_layers.elf
SRC_ORIG  := nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
SRC_PATCHED := $(BUILD_DIR)/nds_nn_examples_cifar10_patched.c

CFLAGS := -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections
CFLAGS += -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS
CFLAGS += -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=$(VLEN)
CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION

INCS := -I$(BUILD_DIR)/shim
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Include
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/internal
INCS += -I.
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Examples/unit_func/include

LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm

# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
	softmax.h activation.h pooling.h fully_connected.h convolution.h)

# Extra flags shared by both perf builds
PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP

.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean

all: $(ELF)

$(ELF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

# Patch: add missing batch=1 argument to riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8 calls
$(SRC_PATCHED): $(SRC_ORIG) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	sed \
	  -e 's/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\(img_buffer[12]\), \(CONV[123]_OUT_DIM\)/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\1, 1, \2/g' \
	  $< > $@

# Generate shim headers
$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
	echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
	mkdir -p $@

$(BUILD_DIR):
	mkdir -p $@

run: $(ELF)
	@echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=$(VLEN) (timeout=$(QEMU_TIMEOUT)s, strace mode)"
	@STRACE_LOG=$(BUILD_DIR)/strace.log; \
	strace -f -e trace=write -o $$STRACE_LOG \
	  $(QEMU) -readconfig $(QEMU_CFG) -M andes_ae350 \
	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=$(VLEN) \
	  -nographic -semihosting -bios $(ELF) > /dev/null 2>&1 & \
	SPID=$$!; \
	sleep $(QEMU_TIMEOUT); \
	kill $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	wait $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	echo "[output]"; \
	grep -E 'write\((9|1),' $$STRACE_LOG \
	  | sed "s/.*write([0-9]*, \"//; s/\\\\n\".*//; s/\".*//"

$(ELF_PERF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

$(ELF_LAYERS): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

perf: $(ELF_PERF)
	@echo "[perf] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_PERF)

perf-layers: $(ELF_LAYERS)
	@echo "[perf-layers] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_LAYERS)

clean:
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

input.content:
################################################################################
# KWS-DNN example - build and run on Andes QEMU / FPGA (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
#
#   make             - build kws_dnn.elf (QEMU/correctness)
#   make run         - run on QEMU (strace mode, captures semihosting output)
#   make perf        - build and run on FPGA, report total inst+cycle count
#   make perf-layers - build and run on FPGA, report per-layer inst+cycle breakdown
#   make clean       - remove build artifacts
################################################################################

LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc

QEMU := /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
QEMU_CFG := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
VLEN := 1024
QEMU_TIMEOUT := 30

BOARD_WRAPPER := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
export MYPC  ?= sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT ?= 1115

BUILD_DIR := build
ELF       := $(BUILD_DIR)/kws_dnn.elf
ELF_PERF  := $(BUILD_DIR)/kws_dnn_perf.elf
ELF_LAYERS := $(BUILD_DIR)/kws_dnn_layers.elf
SRC       := nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c

CFLAGS := -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections
CFLAGS += -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS
CFLAGS += -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=$(VLEN)
CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION

INCS := -I$(BUILD_DIR)/shim
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Include
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/internal
INCS += -I.
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Examples/unit_func/include

LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm

# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
	softmax.h activation.h fully_connected.h)

PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP

.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean

all: $(ELF)

$(ELF): $(SRC) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
	echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
	mkdir -p $@

$(BUILD_DIR):
	mkdir -p $@

run: $(ELF)
	@echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=$(VLEN) (timeout=$(QEMU_TIMEOUT)s, strace mode)"
	@STRACE_LOG=$(BUILD_DIR)/strace.log; \
	strace -f -e trace=write -o $$STRACE_LOG \
	  $(QEMU) -readconfig $(QEMU_CFG) -M andes_ae350 \
	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=$(VLEN) \
	  -nographic -semihosting -bios $(ELF) > /dev/null 2>&1 & \
	SPID=$$!; \
	sleep $(QEMU_TIMEOUT); \
	kill $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	wait $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	echo "[output]"; \
	grep -E 'write\((9|1),' $$STRACE_LOG \
	  | sed "s/.*write([0-9]*, \"//; s/\\\\n\".*//; s/\".*//"

$(ELF_PERF): $(SRC) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

$(ELF_LAYERS): $(SRC) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

perf: $(ELF_PERF)
	@echo "[perf] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_PERF)

perf-layers: $(ELF_LAYERS)
	@echo "[perf-layers] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_LAYERS)

clean:
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

input.content:
# KWS-DNN Baseline Performance

**Model:** ML-KWS-for-MCU DNN ("Hello Edge", arXiv:1711.07128), 4 FC layers, 10x25 MFCC input, 12 classes
**Board:** sw-boards.andestech.com:1115 (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
**Date:** 2026-04-14
**Build:** `-O3 -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DFAST_VERSION`

## Total

| Inst | Cycle |
|---|---|
| 10,012 | 43,387 |

## Per-layer

| Layer | Function | Inst | Cycle |
|---|---|---|---|
| IP1 (250->144) | `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | 3,711 | 16,757 |
| relu1 | `riscv_nn_relu_s8` | 24 | 71 |
| IP2 (144->144) | `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | 2,910 | 10,786 |
| relu2 | `riscv_nn_relu_s8` | 24 | 50 |
| IP3 (144->144) | `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | 2,910 | 11,025 |
| relu3 | `riscv_nn_relu_s8` | 24 | 72 |
| IP4 (144->12) | `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` | 369 | 4,381 |
| softmax | `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast` | 68 | 271 |

Per-layer sum: 10,040 inst / 43,413 cycles (small delta vs total from PFM start/stop overhead).

## Notes

- IP1 dominates (~39% of cycles): 250 input dims with the largest weight matrix (36K q7).
- IP4 cycles/inst ratio (~11.9) is unusually high for only 369 instructions -- small-output FC (12 rows) likely has poor vector utilization tail.
- IP2 vs IP3 are identical shapes (144->144) but IP3 is +239 cycles; noise/board jitter.
- Softmax on 12 elements is negligible.

################################################################################
# KWS-DNN example - build and run on Andes QEMU / FPGA (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
#
#   make             - build kws_dnn.elf (QEMU/correctness)
#   make run         - run on QEMU (strace mode, captures semihosting output)
#   make perf        - build and run on FPGA, report total inst+cycle count
#   make perf-layers - build and run on FPGA, report per-layer inst+cycle breakdown
#   make clean       - remove build artifacts
################################################################################

LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc

QEMU := /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
QEMU_CFG := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
VLEN := 1024
QEMU_TIMEOUT := 30

BOARD_WRAPPER := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
export MYPC  ?= sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT ?= 1115

BUILD_DIR := build
ELF       := $(BUILD_DIR)/kws_dnn.elf
ELF_PERF  := $(BUILD_DIR)/kws_dnn_perf.elf
ELF_LAYERS := $(BUILD_DIR)/kws_dnn_layers.elf
SRC       := nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c

CFLAGS := -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections
CFLAGS += -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS
CFLAGS += -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=$(VLEN)
CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION

INCS := -I$(BUILD_DIR)/shim
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Include
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/internal
INCS += -I.
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Examples/unit_func/include

LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm

# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
	softmax.h activation.h fully_connected.h)

PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP

.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean

all: $(ELF)

$(ELF): $(SRC) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
	echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
	mkdir -p $@

$(BUILD_DIR):
	mkdir -p $@

run: $(ELF)
	@echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=$(VLEN) (timeout=$(QEMU_TIMEOUT)s, strace mode)"
	@STRACE_LOG=$(BUILD_DIR)/strace.log; \
	strace -f -e trace=write -o $$STRACE_LOG \
	  $(QEMU) -readconfig $(QEMU_CFG) -M andes_ae350 \
	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=$(VLEN) \
	  -nographic -semihosting -bios $(ELF) > /dev/null 2>&1 & \
	SPID=$$!; \
	sleep $(QEMU_TIMEOUT); \
	kill $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	wait $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	echo "[output]"; \
	grep -E 'write\((9|1),' $$STRACE_LOG \
	  | sed "s/.*write([0-9]*, \"//; s/\\\\n\".*//; s/\".*//"

$(ELF_PERF): $(SRC) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

$(ELF_LAYERS): $(SRC) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

perf: $(ELF_PERF)
	@echo "[perf] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_PERF)

perf-layers: $(ELF_LAYERS)
	@echo "[perf-layers] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_LAYERS)

clean:
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

################################################################################
# CIFAR-10 CNN example — build and run on Andes QEMU / FPGA (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
#
#   make            — build cifar10.elf (QEMU/correctness)
#   make run        — run on QEMU (strace mode, captures semihosting output)
#   make perf       — build and run on FPGA, report total inst+cycle count
#   make perf-layers — build and run on FPGA, report per-layer inst+cycle breakdown
#   make clean      — remove build artifacts
################################################################################

LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc

QEMU := /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
QEMU_CFG := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
VLEN := 1024
QEMU_TIMEOUT := 30

BOARD_WRAPPER := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
export MYPC  ?= sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT ?= 1115

BUILD_DIR := build
ELF       := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10.elf
ELF_PERF  := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_perf.elf
ELF_LAYERS := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_layers.elf
SRC_ORIG  := nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
SRC_PATCHED := $(BUILD_DIR)/nds_nn_examples_cifar10_patched.c

CFLAGS := -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections
CFLAGS += -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS
CFLAGS += -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=$(VLEN)
CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION

INCS := -I$(BUILD_DIR)/shim
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Include
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/internal
INCS += -I.
INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Examples/unit_func/include

LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm

# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
	softmax.h activation.h pooling.h fully_connected.h convolution.h)

# Extra flags shared by both perf builds
PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP

.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean

all: $(ELF)

$(ELF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

# Patch: add missing batch=1 argument to riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8 calls
$(SRC_PATCHED): $(SRC_ORIG) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	sed \
	  -e 's/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\(img_buffer[12]\), \(CONV[123]_OUT_DIM\)/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\1, 1, \2/g' \
	  $< > $@

# Generate shim headers
$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
	echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@

$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
	mkdir -p $@

$(BUILD_DIR):
	mkdir -p $@

run: $(ELF)
	@echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=$(VLEN) (timeout=$(QEMU_TIMEOUT)s, strace mode)"
	@STRACE_LOG=$(BUILD_DIR)/strace.log; \
	strace -f -e trace=write -o $$STRACE_LOG \
	  $(QEMU) -readconfig $(QEMU_CFG) -M andes_ae350 \
	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=$(VLEN) \
	  -nographic -semihosting -bios $(ELF) > /dev/null 2>&1 & \
	SPID=$$!; \
	sleep $(QEMU_TIMEOUT); \
	kill $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	wait $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
	echo "[output]"; \
	grep -E 'write\((9|1),' $$STRACE_LOG \
	  | sed "s/.*write([0-9]*, \"//; s/\\\\n\".*//; s/\".*//"

$(ELF_PERF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

$(ELF_LAYERS): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
	@echo "Built: $@"

perf: $(ELF_PERF)
	@echo "[perf] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_PERF)

perf-layers: $(ELF_LAYERS)
	@echo "[perf-layers] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
	@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
	PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_LAYERS)

clean:
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

attachment.planContent:
# Plan: KWS-DNN baseline on AX45MPV FPGA

## Context

User wants baseline FPGA cycle counts for the ML-KWS-for-MCU DNN model (the largest model suite in `Examples/`). Goal is to set up a reproducible build+profile flow mirroring the existing cifar10/gru ports under `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/`, producing a `baseline.md` with total and per-layer inst/cycle numbers for future libnn RVV optimization work.

The DNN model is a 4-layer FC keyword-spotting net from the "Hello Edge" paper. CMSIS-NN calls used (`arm_fully_connected_q7`, `arm_relu_q7`, `arm_softmax_q7`) have byte-identical argument lists and semantics to libnn's shift-based `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` / `riscv_nn_relu_s8` / `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast`, so porting is a rename, not a signature rewrite.

## Target layout

Create `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/kws_dnn/` following the cifar10 pattern:

```
kws_dnn/
  Makefile                     # clone of cifar10/Makefile
  nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c    # new standalone driver (no mbed)
  dnn_weights.h                # copied from Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/
  baseline.md                  # results (written after run)
```

## Driver source (`nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c`)

Replicates `Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp` inference in plain C:

- Constants from `dnn.h`: `IN_DIM=250`, `OUT_DIM=12`, `IP1_OUT=IP2_OUT=IP3_OUT=144`, shifts (`IP1_BIAS_LSHIFT=1,IP1_OUT_RSHIFT=7`; `IP2 2/8`; `IP3 2/9`; `IP4 0/6`).
- Input: static MFCC buffer (250 int8). Use pre-computed MFCC from `Deployment/Examples/simple_test/wav_data.h` or zero-fill (baseline cycle count is data-independent for dense FC).
- Call sequence:
  1. `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast(in, IP1_WT, 250, 144, 1, 7, IP1_BIAS, fc1_out, vec_buf)`
  2. `riscv_nn_relu_s8(fc1_out, 144)`
  3. FC 144->144 (IP2), ReLU
  4. FC 144->144 (IP3), ReLU
  5. FC 144->12 (IP4)
  6. `riscv_nn_softmax_s8_fast(fc4_out, 1, 12, out)`
- `vec_buf` q15 scratch sized `2 * max(in_dim)` = 500 q15 elements.

Perf instrumentation: wrap each layer with `startPFM()/stopPFM()` macros guarded by `-DPF_COUNTER`, matching cifar10's pattern (see `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` once the Makefile patches it).

## Makefile

Copy `Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile` verbatim, change:
- `SRC_ORIG := nds_nn_examples_kws_dnn.c`
- `ELF := build/kws_dnn.elf` (and perf/layers variants)
- Drop the `maxpool` sed patch (no maxpool in DNN); keep the shim-header generation (needs `nds_nn_fully_connected.h`, `nds_nn_activation.h`, `nds_nn_softmax.h`).

Targets: `make` (QEMU build), `make run` (QEMU validation), `make perf` (FPGA total), `make perf-layers` (FPGA per-layer).

## Critical files to reference

- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile` - template
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c` - perf-counter instrumentation style
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/baseline.md` - output format
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn.cpp` - layer sequence/shifts
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ML-KWS-for-MCU/Deployment/Source/NN/DNN/dnn_weights.h` - 319KB weight arrays (copy as-is)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Include/riscv_nn_fully_connected.h:241` - `riscv_nn_fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast` signature (matches arm_fully_connected_q7)

## Verification

1. Build libnn: `/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh AX45MPV` (ast530 toolchain on PATH).
2. QEMU correctness: `cd Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/kws_dnn && make run` - expect completion without semihosting errors (the DNN output prediction can be spot-checked but is not required for cycle baseline).
3. FPGA total: `make perf` - reports total inst+cycle.
4. FPGA per-layer: `make perf-layers` - prints 8 rows (4 FC, 3 ReLU, 1 softmax).
5. Record numbers in `kws_dnn/baseline.md` matching cifar10/baseline.md format (Total table + per-layer table with libnn function names). Commit with `[misc]` tag.

Note: FPGA run requires board reservation via `MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com MYPORT=1115` (defaults already in cifar10 Makefile). If the board is unavailable, QEMU run still validates correctness but does not yield cycle counts.
